title: psidium guineense
intro: Psidium guineense, also known as the African guava, is a species of flowering plant in the myrtle family, native to tropical and subtropical regions of West and Central Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic activities. The health benefits of Psidium guineense include the treatment of fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and ointments, with the fruit and leaves being commonly used in traditional medicine.
